task.goal: Bootstrap repo, submodule, and `.planning/` contract on `main`.
Maintain PRD + roadmap alignment (Payload template, Supabase PG, Vercel, three seed emails).
Document the shift from third-party CRM to a Payload-native CRM, lifecycle automation, Stripe billing, and Resend follow-up.
Draft workflow runbooks: lead-to-customer, team login and tool access, CRM touchpoints.
Define "single pane of glass": what lives in Payload admin/routes vs Stripe, Resend, and background jobs; integration map.

commands.command: See DECISIONS D-ops-001 (portal + /ops primary; /admin secondary) and .planning/workflows/crm-and-integrations.md

task.goal: Security framing for shared credentials: Infisical for team secrets; Payload encrypted fields; Supabase/Postgres for sensitive data at rest.
Document internal-docs policy + CRM single-org / mixed-domain model (workflows/*.md, DECISIONS).
Scaffold app from Payload with-vercel-website; verify layout builder and public routes.
Seed and publish the real homepage and core marketing pages for the exterior-cleaning brand.
Quick-edit admin path for reviews, testimonials, and before/after proof. Testimonials: dedicated `testimonials` collection + Testimonials section block (do not place on public pages until approved).
Launch checklist for marketing-site readiness: nav, CTA flow, form coverage, service areas, visual system/theme polish, and mobile QA.
Publish customer-facing trust/support pages in Payload for privacy, terms, refunds, and contact response standards, then link them from the public site.
Define and use a generated-image workflow for hero sections, cards, features, and atmospheric backgrounds so marketing art can be produced quickly and stored cleanly in Payload media or `public/`.

Add an admin-only “Generate image + create media” button on the Payload `media` create screen: admin enters prompt (optionally edits) and clicks generate, which calls OpenAI image generation and creates a new `media` document with the generated upload.

Demo-ready fixtures: `npm run seed` runs `src/endpoints/seed/demo-seed.ts` by default; `npm run seed:baseline` or `SEED_SKIP_DEMO=true` skips demo upserts. Eight `@demo.grimetime.app` personas, CRM + billing + Stripe test sync, demo mode cookie (`grime_demo_mode`) scoping `/ops` + CRM for admins, tours gated per `demo-data-and-flows.md`.

Redesign `/contact` for clarity and stronger visual hierarchy: remove faded treatment, reduce copy density, add brand logo presence in the request card, increase icon-led affordances, and replace loose text-card stacks with compact tabs that drive a single detail panel for long-form guidance.
Connect Payload to Supabase Postgres (adapter + migrations); document env for local and Vercel.
Create and verify the initial admin user in the real Supabase-backed database after approved migrations.

commands.command: Use scripts/bootstrap-admin.ts or the Payload admin login flow after migration approval

task.goal: Prepare Payload MCP and editor wiring so the same local app server can expose `/api/mcp` after approved schema work and local API key setup.

Split customer vs admin access flows so customer login stays customer-only, admin sign-in stays on Payload admin, the admin ops dashboard lives on `/ops`, the customer portal nav stays focused on estimates, invoices, schedule, and account settings, and effective-user auth can render customer scope cleanly when admins impersonate users.

Implement admin impersonation with a floating toolbar that persists across frontend and portal pages, keeps the real admin identity, and swaps the effective user experience without exposing internal views to impersonated non-admins.

commands.command: Use a first-party auth context plus internal admin-only impersonation routes; keep planning aligned with phase 07 company-account CRM scope.

task.goal: Vendor and wire an image-generation MCP server with a repo-local Python environment, root `.env` config passthrough, Cursor/Codex setup, and a dev-process hook so generated imagery becomes part of the normal content workflow.
Keep deploys deterministic by documenting the Payload/Vercel env contract, pinning Node on Vercel, and making Payload bootable during prerender when `PAYLOAD_SECRET` is accidentally missing.
Keep Vercel production deploys on Webpack when Turbopack packaging produces an ESM/CommonJS launcher mismatch under the repo's module settings.

commands.command: Use `next build --webpack` in package.json until the current deploy/runtime mismatch is no longer present.

task.goal: Modernize customer auth by introducing Supabase-backed email/password, magic-link, reset-password, claim-account, and company-invite flows while keeping Payload admin auth for staff and preserving Payload `users` as the portal authorization/profile record.
Lead forms + contact storage; shared first-party quote/contact/schedule/support submission path; Payload-native CRM records with no third-party CRM dependency.

commands.command: Payload form-submissions, internal CRM entities, and sequence enrollment design

task.goal: Calendar booking and schedule request flow - use first-party forms with shared validation/helpers, customer portal schedule-change requests, recurring-plan cadence, and integrate the lead into Payload plus internal follow-up state.
Calls / quote requests intake, internal activity timeline, and Resend-based outbound email visibility plan.
Promote Payload quotes into an in-house opportunity / pipeline model so relationship management and follow-up live inside Payload instead of a third-party CRM.
Build the first internal CRM workspace in Payload: leads, accounts, contacts, opportunities, activities, tasks, and follow-up queue powering `/ops` and future account views.

Customer portal account surfaces: estimates from Payload quotes, invoices, recurring service plans, service appointments, and self-serve account/contact updates with no docs/admin clutter in customer nav.

commands.command: See src/app/(portal)/dashboard/page.tsx, estimates/page.tsx, invoices/page.tsx, service-schedule/page.tsx, account/page.tsx

task.goal: Improve customer activation and attention capture: account-claim links from quote/invoice/schedule emails, clearer first-login states, and easier entry into the portal when a customer already exists in CRM but has not claimed access yet. (2026-03-27: dashboard welcome + profile CTA; estimates page claim hint.)
Quoting system: internal Payload collection + env gate, plus draftable/publishable quote-settings globals that power the public instant quote math and service toggles.
Build and document the internal dashboard command center: KPI cards, chart, daily board, scorecard, milestones, asset guidance, and first-party operating views powered by Payload data.
Define KPI formulas and replace placeholder projected revenue, MRR, margin, and milestone thresholds with real business targets before moving them into editable app data.
Promote the dashboard scorecard from static docs/config into editable internal app data once schema and migrations are approved.

Payload CRUD for growth milestones and ops asset inventory (`growth-milestones`, `ops-asset-ladder-items`); portal tabs read from DB; Internal ops targets extended for KPI/scorecard tooltips and chart pipeline note.

Ops portal layout fix (single right column for all TabsContent), KPI row above command center, liabilities + scorecard rows collections with merge fallbacks, admin customer `/dashboard` preview + dual sidebar nav, optional `PORTAL_ADMIN_ONLY`, planning/docs updates, and first-party day-board wiring (D-ops-003).

commands.command: migration 20260325_005955_ops_liabilities_and_scorecard_rows; src/components/data-table.tsx, AdminDashboardView, app-sidebar; src/collections/OpsLiabilityItems, OpsScorecardRows; src/lib/ops/mergeScorecard.ts

task.goal: Wire ops scorecard/liability CRUD into the live dashboard, seed default rows so `/ops` is not empty, add scheduled-job reads to the day-board calendar, and move dashboard detail panels into the left rail instead of overlay sheets.

Refactor `/ops` KPI cards to compact number-first presentation with shared info-icon tooltips that explain formula, composition, and data freshness; reduce paragraph copy and keep card height/scan density consistent.

Portal guided tours (react-joyride): staff vs customer registry, localStorage completion, Ops overview session autolaunch for real admins, staff deep tours (CRM, Today, scorecard/liabilities, assets, milestones, portal Docs) with `?tab=` / path routing; customer surface tours (dashboard, estimates, invoices, schedule, account); sidebar launcher + portal doc alignment; marketing-site instant-quote tour (`SiteTourProvider`, button-only entry). Align with `demo-data-and-flows.md` and shell refactors (07-11, 07-12, 07-14) without breaking `data-tour` targets. QA richness gated by BLOCKERS `BLK-demo-seed-001` (`03-09`).

Former backlog from `06-10` — delivered: `staff-milestones`, `staff-portal-docs`, `customer-estimates`, `customer-invoices`, `customer-schedule`, `customer-account`, site `public-instant-quote` (explicit button only). Out of scope: Payload admin shortcuts tour. AI assistant tour deferred per BLOCKERS `BLK-tour-ai-001` (phase `08`).

Define and implement the first-party CRM schema: leads, accounts, contacts, opportunities, activities, tasks, and sequence enrollments linked to quotes, invoices, service plans, appointments, and users, including company-account membership that can power customer login scope.

Build internal CRM API routes and query helpers for queues, search, list/detail views, owner assignment, stale follow-up detection, activity timeline writes, lead qualification/disqualification, and compact dashboard summaries.

Replace placeholder ops surfaces with a compact CRM workspace using shared tables, list rows, detail panes, status badges, icons, tooltips, priority sorting, owner/commercial filters, and stale-contact follow-up indicators.

Implement Stripe-backed billing sync: webhook ingestion, idempotent invoice/subscription reconciliation, customer billing actions, hosted Stripe invoice/portal flows, commercial terms billing, portal-visible payment state, and compact admin billing controls that do not rely on raw collection editors for daily work.

Implement the fastest first-party sequence engine path with Payload jobs and Resend templates for lead follow-up, quote reminders, appointment confirmations, and billing reminders delivered to each worker's normal inbox (personal or company email) before optional mailbox OAuth integrations.
Seed realistic demo data and add unit, integration, and Playwright coverage for CRM flows, Stripe webhook handling, and customer portal account/billing/schedule behavior.

commands.command: cmd /c npm run test:int && cmd /c npm run test:e2e

task.goal: Define the day-to-day operating model: role matrix, ownership rules, queue priorities, SLA timers (including support/refund classes), and required next-action fields so staff always know what to do each day.

Enforce strict data/UI separation boundaries: public and customer views only see allowed account-scoped data; admin-only docs, queues, and cross-user/company visibility remain staff-only, including impersonation-safe guards.
Operationalize local MCP usage for Codex/Cursor with a repeatable verification checklist (endpoint reachable, key valid, server list healthy, and image-gen stdio launchable from repo scripts).
Design and implement discount policy support for account-wide defaults and user-level overrides, with clear precedence, audit history, and safe staff-only controls.
Refactor the portal shell to conventional shadcn app-layout patterns so sidebar, inset content, and admin impersonation toolbar do not overlap; ensure the toolbar remains top-layer and visible.

Keep `/ops` as the KPI-first staff dashboard, move dense daily work into `/ops/workspace`, make billing follow-up a first-class ops-workspace tab, and keep legacy `/ops/*` section routes only as compatibility redirects into that single non-overlapping workspace.

commands.command: See src/app/(portal)/ops/page.tsx, src/app/(portal)/ops/workspace/page.tsx, src/app/(portal)/ops/*/page.tsx redirects, src/components/portal/command-center/*

task.goal: Implement deterministic preview identity and nav labeling: `Customer home` should use a non-admin `test_user` effective context, show context labels in sidebar (`(admin)` / `(test_user)`), and hide admin-only docs/settings when viewing customer scope.

Adopt and enforce frontend/code-quality conventions: stronger icon usage where helpful, reduce copy-heavy/faded UI patterns, extract large files, centralize repeated literals into constants/typed unions, formalize reusable utilities across ops/customer surfaces, and standardize server-first plus TanStack Query client-cache patterns for live mutable workflows.

Execute the refactor extraction roadmap toward the target folder structure for ops/auth/crm/dashboard modules, including prioritized large-file splits, presenter/policy extraction, and typed query-param utilities for `/ops/*` routing.

Architecture + DECISION for AI ops assistant beta: scope (business operations only), env feature flag (`AI_OPS_ASSISTANT_ENABLED` or similar), staff/admin gate, OpenAI + Vercel AI SDK baseline, no customer-facing chat until promoted, explicit PII/logging redaction policy.

RAG: embeddings pipeline for approved docs (portal `src/content/docs/`, selected playbooks), vector storage (e.g. Supabase `pgvector` or managed index), re-ingest on doc change, retrieval contracts for quote/CRM terminology.

Staff UI: Assistant UI + Tool UI chat shell in portal (or `/ops` subtree), **Beta** label, streaming via Vercel AI SDK, tool calls to internal read-only APIs (quotes, CRM search) behind explicit allowlists — no broad write access in v1.

Optional multimodal: screenshot / image upload → vision extraction of phone numbers and notes → structured handoff to CRM lead/quote flows; rate limits; user confirmation before any write; document field use case (contacts in phone notes).
Operational guardrails: audit logging, cost/rate limits, retention policy for chat transcripts, kill switch via env, security review before enabling in production org.
Tests: integration tests for retrieval + tool wiring; gated Playwright smoke for beta UI when flag on.
DECISION D-seed-001 + workflow stub `.planning/workflows/seed-system-and-cli.md` (phase `09` PLAN/SUMMARY remain source of truth).
Module graph + `registerModule` pattern; extract foundation slice from `src/endpoints/seed/index.ts` without changing default full-graph seed initially.
Media module: uploads, dedup / overwrite warnings, optional strict mode; map referencing pages when replacing media.
Pages/layout module: home hero, service grids, gallery slots; fix duplicate media assignments using redundancy report.
Demo submodules (CRM, billing, marketing personas) under agreed folder + `SEED_SKIP_DEMO` mapping to subgraph.
CLI (`npm run grimetime`): **shipped** — `seed pull|delete`, `check|summary --json`, guarded `delete all`. **Remaining** — `--dry-run`/`push` flag polish, `--only`, `--no-input`, `report`/`plan`.

commands.command: src/cli/grimetime.ts, src/cli/seed/seed-commands.ts, src/cli/lib/seed-pull.ts, src/cli/lib/seed-delete.ts, src/cli/lib/seed-check.ts, scripts/seed.ts; DECISION D-seed-001

task.goal: Tests: Vitest for planner + redundancy analyzer; integration hooks for module-scoped seed; e2e doc for Playwright demo profile. **Started:** `tests/int/seed/expectations-alignment.int.spec.ts`.
